summary: Iteration 3 (DICOM export + 512px server-side downscale) tested end-to-end. All 5 new backend tests pass (DICOM happy path with full pydicom parsing of PatientID/Modality/SeriesDescription/ImageComments/private tag (0x0099,0x1001)/Rows/Columns<=512, 404 on unknown id, large-image downscale to longest-side 512, small-image preservation, PDF regression). Frontend HistoryPage verified live: download-report-{id}, download-dicom-{id}, delete-{id} all render; DICOM click triggers a real .dcm download (qsvm-<id8>.dcm) and 'DICOM downloaded' toast fires. ResultsPanel DICOM button verified by code review (uses same downloadDicom helper as History which is confirmed working).
critical_code_review_comments: dicom_export.py uses datetime.utcnow() (deprecated in Py 3.12+); switch to datetime.now(timezone.utc). Not a blocker.
build_dicom catches all decode failures and emits a 1x1 black DICOM; consider 422 if upstream image is missing entirely to surface data corruption.
seed_data_creation: Tests self-create scans via POST /api/predict and clean up via DELETE /api/history/{id}.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_qsvm_dicom.py (iteration 3). Mock /api/predict takes ~5-10s so each scan-creation test is slow; full DICOM suite ~30s. Private DICOM tag access: ds[(0x0099, 0x1001)].value with creator 'QSVM NEURODX' registered via private_block. Frontend history page shows DICOM/PDF/Delete buttons per row with the documented testids; clicking DICOM in Playwright works using page.expect_download().
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found in iteration 3.
